what: Automatically create and style ggplot2 charts
title: Automatically create and style ggplot2 charts
text: ggauto opinionated ggplot2 extension package automatically choose best chart type styling, based types values data. [!WARNING] don’t like () opinionated choices package, make fork create version. Bug reports /fixes extremely welcome things don’t work, stylistic changes personal preferences addressed. terms styling, defaults differ ggplot2 following ways: use white background improve contrast. Larger text aligned horizontally improve readability. Improved styling title subtitle, including automatic text wrapping long text. Colours likely accessible, using Paul Tol’s palettes. Combined use either shapes direct labels alongside colour improve accessibility. Symmetric y axis, 0 included data (chart types), enable comparison. Errors users try make spaghetti line charts.

title: Automatically create and style ggplot2 charts
text: can install development version ggauto GitHub : Load package:

previous_headings: Mapping data types to chart types
title: Automatically create and style ggplot2 charts
text: available data types based scale_x/y_ options ggplot2: Continuous Discrete (categorical) Date (including time datetime)

title: Automatically create and style ggplot2 charts
text: Pass columns data vectors:  Note symmetric x-axis, added 0 exists range x values. Since output ggauto() simply ggplot2 chart, can override don’t want :  ’ll get warning say replacing existing scale can ignore ’s ’re trying ! can title, subtitle, caption, labels labs() function ggplot2 normally , directly using arguments ggauto(). latter recommended arguments used little abnormally implement styling. can add markdown formatting title, subtitle, caption:
code: set.seed(123) plot_data <- data.frame(   v1 = rnorm(50, 1) ) ggauto(plot_data$v1) library(ggplot2) ggauto(plot_data$v1) +   scale_x_continuous() #> Scale for x is already present. #> Adding another scale for x, which will replace the existing scale. ggauto(plot_data$v1,   title = "Descriptive title goes here",   subtitle = "More information about what's in the chart which can be a really, really long sentence that will wrap onto multiple lines automatically.",   caption = "**Source**: where the data is from",   xlab = "Nice variable name" )

title: Automatically create and style ggplot2 charts
text: Let’s create basic dataset two continuous variables:  Compare basic, default scatter plot ggplot2:
code: set.seed(123) plot_data <- data.frame(   v1 = runif(20),   v2 = runif(20, -5, 5) ) ggauto(plot_data$v1, plot_data$v2) ggplot(   data = plot_data,   mapping = aes(x = v1, y = v2) ) +   geom_point()

title: Automatically create and style ggplot2 charts
code: set.seed(123) plot_data <- data.frame(   v1 = runif(20),   v2 = runif(20, -5, 5),   v3 = sample(LETTERS[1:4], 20, replace = TRUE) ) ggauto(plot_data$v1, plot_data$v2, plot_data$v3)

what: One date variable, one continuous variable
title: Automatically create and style ggplot2 charts
code: set.seed(123) plot_data <- data.frame(   v1 = seq(     as.Date("01-01-2024", tryFormats = "%d-%m-%Y"),     as.Date("01-12-2024", tryFormats = "%d-%m-%Y"),     by = "months"   ),   v2 = runif(12, -5, 5) ) ggauto(plot_data$v1, plot_data$v2)

what: One date variable, one continuous variable, one discrete variable
title: Automatically create and style ggplot2 charts
text: Category labels added end line automatically. moment, need manually adjust x axis make sure fit (depending length category labels).  try use 6 colours, error returned. Future iterations package may automatically use small multiples (facets) situation.
code: set.seed(123) plot_data <- data.frame(   v1 = rep(seq(     as.Date("01-01-2024", tryFormats = "%d-%m-%Y"),     as.Date("01-12-2024", tryFormats = "%d-%m-%Y"),     by = "months"   ), each = 4),   v2 = runif(48),   v3 = rep(LETTERS[1:4], times = 12) ) ggauto(plot_data$v1, plot_data$v2, plot_data$v3) +   scale_x_date(limits = as.Date(c("01-01-2024", "31-12-2024"),     tryFormats = "%d-%m-%Y"   )) set.seed(123) plot_data <- data.frame(   v1 = rep(seq(     as.Date("01-01-2024", tryFormats = "%d-%m-%Y"),     as.Date("01-12-2024", tryFormats = "%d-%m-%Y"),     by = "months"   ), each = 7),   v2 = runif(84),   v3 = rep(LETTERS[1:7], times = 12) ) ggauto(plot_data$v1, plot_data$v2, plot_data$v3) #> Error in `ggauto()`: #> ! You cannot use more than 6 colours.

title: Automatically create and style ggplot2 charts
text: character variable, sorted highest lowest:  factor variable, order respected:
code: set.seed(123) plot_data <- data.frame(   v1 = sample(LETTERS[1:6], 20, replace = TRUE) ) ggauto(plot_data$v1) plot_data$v1 <-factor(plot_data$v1, levels = LETTERS[1:6]) ggauto(plot_data$v1)

what: One discrete variable, one continuous variable
title: Automatically create and style ggplot2 charts
text: one continuous value discrete variable, bar chart created e.g. ’ve pre-computed counts:  multiple continuous values discrete variable, raincloud plot created:
code: set.seed(123) plot_data <- data.frame(   v1 = LETTERS[1:6],   v2 = rpois(6, 6) ) ggauto(plot_data$v1, plot_data$v2) set.seed(123) plot_data <- data.frame(   v1 = rep(LETTERS[1:3], each = 25),   v2 = rnorm(75) ) ggauto(plot_data$v1, plot_data$v2)

title: Automatic ggplot plot — ggauto
text: Automatically create appropriate ggplot2 chart type based classes provided variables.

title: Automatic ggplot plot — ggauto
code: ggauto(   var1 = NULL,   var2 = NULL,   var3 = NULL,   xlab = "x",   ylab = "y",   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   caption = NULL,   base_size = 14,   base_family = "sans" )

title: Automatic ggplot plot — ggauto
text: var1 First variable. var2 Optional second variable. var3 Optional third variable. xlab Label x-axis. Defaults "x". ylab Label y-axis. Defaults "y". title Optional plot title. subtitle Optional plot subtitle. caption Optional plot caption. base_size Base font size. Defaults 14. base_family Base font family. Defaults "sans".
